invariant: Source lifecycle, actor visibility, and sensitivity policy jointly govern trust, provenance rendering, operational channels, and customer-facing output eligibility.
pass_fail_definition: PASS = bundle-17 is mandatory; active, unavailable, revoked, expired, restricted, and stale source states are substrate-backed; downstream source_asof beats fresh wrapper fetch time; Tauri and MCP render through shared actor/surface policy; every RenderPolicyChannel::all() channel rejects revoked or restricted source detail; redaction keeps safe source class, lifecycle state, timestamp posture, and reason; logs and telemetry stay low-detail; internal-only content never becomes customer-facing text. FAIL = any restricted, revoked, stale, or internal-only detail leaks through a channel, MCP detail bypasses actor filtering, a new channel is unclassified, or fixture data is not synthetic.

fixture_design_notes.state_sql: Seeds one synthetic account, one synthetic meeting, source lifecycle rows, channel policy rows, and claims for disconnected Google, revoked Slack/Gong/Zendesk, restricted Glean downstream, stale downstream object behind fresh wrapper fetch, and an internal-only note near a public suggestion.
fixture_design_notes.provider_replay: Includes attempted unsafe public suggestions from internal-only and revoked sources so tests assert rejection instead of absence by omission.
fixture_design_notes.external_replay: Pins wrapper fetch time versus downstream object source_asof for Clay/Glean and actor visibility for Tauri user versus MCP agent.
fixture_design_notes.expected_output: Stores the channel sweep oracle keyed by RenderPolicyChannel::all() names and safe surface outputs.
fixture_design_notes.expected_provenance: Stores useful redaction summary, masked provenance shape, actor scope contract, and forbidden detail tokens.
fixture_design_notes.expected_state: Asserts source lifecycle state, invalidation/degradation, trust bands, redaction, and no-leak outcomes.
